rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical isomerizing allylation and allenylation of aldehydes with unactivated alkenes and alkynes via cobalt-salen-mediated hydrogen evolution generating allylic/propargylic carbanions
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of Co(II)-salen to Co(I), which deprotonates the allylic/propargylic C-H substrate to form Co(III)-H; further SET reduction gives Co(II)-H that releases H2, shifting the dissociation equilibrium to slowly release the carbanion nucleophile (indirect HER)
electron_flow: Cathode reduces Co(II)-salen to Co(I), which deprotonates the weakly acidic allylic/propargylic C-H of the alkene/alkyne to give Co(III)-H and a carbanion; a second electron converts Co(III)-H to Co(II)-H, releasing H2 and regenerating Co(II). The slowly released carbanion then undergoes nucleophilic addition to benzaldehyde via the kinetically and thermodynamically favored TS1(E) to give the linear (E)-allylation or allenylation product.
rate_determining_step: Deprotonation/proton transfer of the allylic (or propargylic) C-H bond during the HER step (supported by secondary KIE in parallel experiments and larger KIE in intermolecular competition)

probe_reagents.name: Cyclopropyl-substituted allyl/alkyne and aldehyde substrates (radical clock)
probe_reagents.observation: Products 3bm-3br and 5bd-5be obtained with cyclopropyl ring intact; no ring-opening or cyclization, excluding a radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: Allylbenzene-d2 (1be-d2)
probe_reagents.observation: Parallel kH/kD = 1.3, intermolecular competition kH/kD = 3.3; secondary KIE consistent with H/D exchange of carbanion and proton transfer in HER

probe_reagents.name: NaH in DMF (stoichiometric base mimic)
probe_reagents.observation: With allylbenzene + PhCHO gives isomerizing allylation product 3a in 62% yield (linear E); fails for 4a (alkyne) due to lower acidity, supporting carbanion intermediate for allylation

probe_reagents.name: Acidic additives (HOAc, MeOH, H2O)
probe_reagents.observation: Addition of acid dramatically decreases yield (trace-40%) due to competing HER of the acid, supporting an anionic/HER-driven mechanism

probe_reagents.name: DABCO (CV probe)
probe_reagents.observation: DABCO is cathodically inert but enhances Co(II)/Co(III) and Co(I)/Co(II) redox peaks of salen catalyst I; required additive for catalysis

notes: Two mechanisms are proposed: (favored) indirect HER via Co(II)-salen mediator generating Co(I)/Co(III)-H/Co(II)-H that deprotonates substrate, and (alternative) direct HER where the cathode reduces protons from the weakly acidic substrate directly. CV shows substrate (allylbenzene Ered = -1.78 V) is more reducible than aldehydes (-1.96 to -2.74 V), but the cathode potential is held more positive than aldehyde reduction to avoid the side reaction (PhCHO -> PhCH2OH or pinacol). DFT (PBE0+D3(BJ)/ma-def2-TZVPP//B3LYP/Def2-SVP) shows TS1(E) is kinetically (4.3 vs 6.0/8.9 kcal/mol) and thermodynamically (-2.8 vs -0.5/+3.3 kcal/mol) favored, rationalizing the unusual linear (E)-selectivity over the branched product.
